updates.title: Introducing Claude for Teachers
updates.summary: Anthropic launched Claude for Teachers on July 14, 2026, a free product for verified K-12 educators in the US that provides access to premium Claude capabilities, a library of teaching skills grounded in learning science, and evidence-based curricula (OpenSciEd, Illustrative Mathematics) mapped to academic standards across all 50 states. Teachers can use it to plan lessons, differentiate materials for individual learners, and generate classroom-ready content — all without Anthropic training its models on teacher or student data. Verified educators who sign up by June 30, 2027 receive one full year of free access, and the platform complies with FERPA through a K-12 data processing agreement co-developed with the American Federation of Teachers.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed by Anthropic official announcement (anthropic.com/news/claude-for-teachers) and independently covered by Chalkbeat, The Hill, Forbes, 9to5Mac, EdSurge, and Benzinga — all dating publication to July 14, 2026. Privacy commitments (no model training on teacher conversations, FERPA alignment) are stated in Anthropic's own announcement. No conflicting claims or red flags found across sources.

updates.title: Claude Enterprise Organization Management via Admin API (Beta)
updates.summary: Anthropic released a new beta capability on July 13, 2026, enabling enterprise administrators to manage their Claude Enterprise organizations directly through the Admin API using the header `anthropic-beta: ce-user-management-2026-07-13`. The feature supports listing members, changing roles, removing members, and managing groups and custom roles programmatically. This reduces the dependency on the Claude dashboard for large-scale user management and gives IT and DevOps teams full API-level control over access and permissions in Claude Enterprise deployments.
updates.accuracy_notes: Confirmed via Anthropic's official release notes (docs.anthropic.com/en/release-notes) retrieved through web search. The specific beta header `ce-user-management-2026-07-13` is consistent with Anthropic's established beta-header naming convention. No conflicting information found. Audience is primarily enterprise developers and IT admins — narrower than consumer news but highly verifiable.
